title: feat: mirror GitHub repo state into D1
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Mirrors all PRs, issues, and PR→issue close edges from pollinations/pollinations into D1 (`gh_pull_requests`, `gh_issues`, `gh_pr_closing_issues`)
- Runs on the existing `*/15` Worker cron; fail-soft (logged, never breaks tier refill)
- GitHub App auth via WebCrypto only (PKCS#1→PKCS#8 wrap); all-GraphQL fetch
- Generic snapshot — reusable substrate for more data sources later
- Chunked upserts bounded by row count AND byte size to avoid SQLITE_TOOBIG on large issue bodies
- Stale-row reap: rows not seen in a run are deleted
- Verified end-to-end on staging cron: 5021 PRs / 6604 issues / 1816 edges, no errors
- 8/8 unit tests, biome + tsc clean

Note: needs `GITHUB_APP_ID` + `GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY` Worker secrets (present on staging; not yet on prod).

gist.headline: Mirroring the Hive
gist.blurb: We've built a cozy little bridge between GitHub and our database. All our open-source history is now safely mirrored in D1!
gist.summary: Added a 15-minute cron job to mirror all GitHub PRs, issues, and closing edges into the D1 database using chunked upserts and WebCrypto authentication.
gist.impact: This internal infrastructure lays the groundwork for future automated contributor rewards and deeper GitHub integration without hitting API rate limits.
gist.visual_concept: Nomnom creature eating GitHub documents and storing them in a D1 database server rack, assisted by Polly the bee.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round tan Nomnom creature happily eating little pixelated GitHub issue documents, while the cute bee mascot organizes them into a glowing lime green (#ecf874) retro server rack. Soft pastel lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
